summary: The average time to resolve a critical IT incident nearly doubled from 6 hours in FY22 to 11 in FY23, which OTI attributed to greater dependence on third-party telecommunications and cloud-system vendors (prompting an application-modernization project for 28 legacy systems). It then dropped back to about 7 hours in FY24-FY26 as OTI reported operational changes that reduced reliance on those outside vendors.
oneLiner: increasing overall, though it dropped back after OTI reduced vendor dependence
